content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures can be repurposed to create a dynamic "novelty reservoir" that automatically adjusts the intrinsic reward signal for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM/RND frameworks), solving their long-standing problem of over-saturation on uninformative novel distractors. This memory-augmented reward design weights novel experiences by how much they improve the agent’s future downstream task performance, rather than rewarding prediction error alone.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
